action.hypothesis: The routed soft-codebook two-stage family has now improved both `K=8` and `K=16`, so the next highest-value test is whether the same routing advantage extends to `K=24`, where the current frontier is still owned by the non-routed variant.
action.summary: Run one parameter-only follow-up at `K=24` with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. This cleanly maps whether the new family is becoming the dominant low/mid-K backbone rather than a narrow win at only `K=8` and `K=16`.
action.self_review: This is a straightforward exploitation step with a single changed dimension (`K`). It directly follows the observed frontier gains and avoids unnecessary code churn. The main risk is that routing helps only in tighter-budget regimes and may become neutral or harmful by `K=24`, but that is precisely what this run will resolve.
notes_to_memory: r93 should extend the routed soft-codebook family sweep to `K=24` after frontier wins at `K=8` and `K=16`.
Keep the recipe fixed and change only `K` so the result cleanly measures whether routing remains helpful in the mid-K regime.
A `K=24` win would strongly suggest the routed soft-codebook family is the new EF=12 backbone across multiple frontier slots.
next_hypotheses: If `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` wins at `K=24`, test it at `K=32` under the same recipe to see whether routing can displace the current high end of the EF=12 frontier as well.
If it is close but slightly behind at `K=24`, consider a small routed-temperature adjustment as the first targeted tuning step for this family.
If it clearly loses at `K=24`, conclude that the routed advantage likely tops out by `K=16` and focus future routed-family work on low-K slots only.

This cleanly maps whether the new family is becoming the dominant low/mid-K backbone rather than a narrow win at only `K=8` and `K=16`.

The main risk is that routing helps only in tighter-budget regimes and may become neutral or harmful by `K=24`, but that is precisely what this run will resolve.
